channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Processed 500,000 Job Applications With AI. Here Is What the Data Actually Shows.
item.description: Full Transparency Disclaimer: I am the founder of Jobloo. After testing tools like LazyApply and Sonara and watching their browser extensions trigger ATS spam filters, I spent 2 years engineering...

item.title: How does your domain know your user?
item.description: Almost every business application needs to answer a question that sounds simple: who is the current user? But "user" isn't one thing. Authorization needs to know what you're allowed to do....

item.title: Two resolvers, four entry points
item.description: Part 2: Two resolvers, four entry points In part 1 we established the shape of the problem: handlers want IIdentity and IPrincipal handed to them as plain parameters, but something above the...

item.title: JavaScript still can't ship a full-stack module
item.description: Imagine if there were a way for us to somehow ship a full-stack package that you could plug into your app. Client code, backend code, webhooks, and database models all wired up and ready to go. I...

item.title: How I got 570k Reddit impressions for my side project using data journalism posts
item.description: Earlier this year I built PriceSquirrel, an EU-wide PC hardware price comparison platform. I had no marketing budget, no existing audience, and no connections in the PC hardware world. Three...

item.title: How We Prevented Magento Checkout Deadlocks for a Big Retailer During Traffic Surges
item.description: Handling checkout deadlocks during peak traffic is a critical challenge for enterprise Magento merchants. In this post, I’ll share how we tackled Magento checkout deadlocks for a major retailer...

item.title: Buscando 5 beta testers para FitzWatch — el status page que construí en Fitz
item.description: TL;DR Construí fitzwatch (https://www.fitzwatch.com), un status page para apps en prod estilo Statuspage de Atlassian pero más liviano y barato. El producto está vivo y operacional. Antes de...

item.title: Route Phone Calls to an AI Agent With the Telnyx Voice API
item.description: Voice AI agents are transforming customer service, sales, and support. But before an AI can talk to a caller, you need the voice infrastructure layer — a way to receive inbound phone calls,...

item.title: Change failure rate is up 30% — here's how to measure yours in an afternoon
item.description: A practitioner's guide to calculating your team's CFR without a vendor platform — the DORA formula, the SQL, and the AI-assisted vs human-authored split nobody is publishing yet. Cortex's 2026...

item.title: Backstage alternatives in 2026: first ask why you wanted Backstage
item.description: Every "Backstage alternatives" roundup lists the same five portals. None of them asks the question that decides which alternative is right: what job sent you looking in the first place? A senior...

item.title: The Download: metric weaknesses and AI elephant warnings
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The inevitable weakness of metrics There are plenty of...

item.title: How to end a TV show
item.description: Ending any story is hard, but that's especially true of mystery-packed TV shows. Series like Lost initially hook viewers with constantly building secrets and questions, to the point that they can...

item.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
item.description: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers. Although no...

item.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
item.description: A Russian advanced persistent threat (APT) group has continued to evolve and expand its malware arsenal as part of its ongoing cyber onslaught against Ukraine throughout 2025. Slovakian...

item.title: Comcast is splitting in two
item.description: Comcast has announced plans to separate itself into two publicly traded companies, spinning off its NBCUniversal and Sky broadcasting arms. The shake up aims to protect the media conglomerate's...

item.title: The war against ‘woke’ could end US science as we know it
item.description: A sneaky rule change has the potential to blow up scientific research in the United States. But there's still time to fight it. On May 29th, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) issued a...

item.title: Robot Police Officers
item.description: We’ve taken one small step towards robot police officers: a drone capable of disarming a suspect: In a June 22 video posted on the Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office’s Instagram page, an officer...

item.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
item.description: Microsoft has shut down a long-running malicious extension operation on the Edge Add-ons store that hid its payloads inside ordinary image and font files, then woke up days after install to steal...

item.title: This Humanoid Robot Is a Terrifyingly Competent Office Intern
item.description: Flexion Robotics, a startup founded by ex-Nvidia engineers, has a clever way of training robots to do useful work.

item.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
item.description: A public proof-of-concept is now out for CVE-2026-55200, a critical flaw in libssh2 that lets a malicious or compromised SSH server trigger memory corruption on a connecting client, with possible...

item.title: Mapping Europe’s AI Workforce Opportunity
item.description: A new OpenAI report maps how AI could reshape jobs across the EU, highlighting which occupations may face automation, growth, or workflow changes.

item.title: Hijacked npm and Go Packages Use VS Code Tasks to Deploy Python Infostealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered two hijacked npm packages and a cluster of Go packages that are designed to deploy a Python-based information stealer on compromised Windows, Linux, and...

item.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
item.description: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models...

item.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
item.description: Suno has ambitions to be more than just a toy to churn out AI slop, it also wants to be a streaming destination and to break new artists. Spark is their new incubator program for independent...

item.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
item.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

item.title: China claims the world’s fastest supercomputer
item.description: Despite trade restrictions, China has reclaimed the title of the world's fastest supercomputer for the first time since 2018. LineShine has pushed El Capitan out of number one on the TOP500...

item.title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
item.description: HP Inc. scales its OpenAI Frontier partnership to deploy AI across customer experiences, software development, and enterprise operations.

item.title: The Cube is Jim Henson’s little-known proto-Black Mirror masterpiece
item.description: I'm sure we're all familiar with Dark Crystal, so we know that Jim Henson can be weird and tackle slightly more mature subject matter. But there is little in his oeuvre that is quite as...

item.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
item.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

item.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht was facing arson charges for setting a fire on New Year's Day in 2025, which became one of the deadliest wildfires in LA history. To make their case, prosecutors turned to...
